messages.content: How can I find peace of mind?

sources.sanskrit: युञ्जन्नेवं सदाऽऽत्मानं योगी नियतमानसः। | शान्तिं निर्वाणपरमां मत्संस्थामधिगच्छति
sources.english: Thus, always keeping the mind balanced, the yogi, with the mind controlled, attains the peace abiding in Me, culminating in liberation.
sources.hindi: ।।6.15।। नियत मनवाला योगी मनको इस तरहसे सदा परमात्मामें लगाता हुआ मेरेमें सम्यक् स्थितिवाली जो निर्वाणपरमा शान्ति है, उसको प्राप्त हो जाता है।

sources.sanskrit: नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना। | न चाभावयतः शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुतः सुखम्
sources.english: There is no knowledge of the Self for the unsteady, and no meditation is possible for the unsteady, and no peace for the unmeditative, and how can there be happiness for one who has no peace?
sources.hindi: ।।2.66।। जिसके मन-इन्द्रियाँ संयमित नहीं हैं, ऐसे मनुष्यकी व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धि नहीं होती और व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धि न होनेसे उसमें कर्तव्यपरायणताकी भावना नहीं होती। ऐसी भावना न होनेसे उसको शान्ति नहीं मिलती। फिर शान्तिरहित मनुष्यको सुख कैसे मिल सकता है?

sources.sanskrit: यदा विनियतं चित्तमात्मन्येवावतिष्ठते। | निःस्पृहः सर्वकामेभ्यो युक्त इत्युच्यते तदा
sources.english: When the perfectly controlled mind rests in the Self alone, free from longing for any of the objects of desire, then it is said, 'He is united'.
sources.hindi: ।।6.18।। वशमें किया हुआ चित्त जिस कालमें अपने स्वरूपमें ही स्थित हो जाता है और स्वयं सम्पूर्ण पदार्थोंसे निःस्पृह हो जाता है, उस कालमें वह योगी है - ऐसा कहा जाता है।

sources.sanskrit: चेतसा सर्वकर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्परः।बुद्धियोगमुपाश्रित्य मच्चित्तः सततं भव
sources.english: Mentally renouncing all actions in Me, having Me as the highest goal, and resorting to the yoga of discrimination, do thou ever fix thy mind on Me.
sources.hindi: ।।18.57।।चित्तसे सम्पूर्ण कर्म मुझमें अर्पण करके, मेरे परायण होकर तथा समताका आश्रय लेकर निरन्तर मुझमें चित्तवाला हो जा।

sources.sanskrit: यत्रोपरमते चित्तं निरुद्धं योगसेवया। | यत्र चैवात्मनाऽऽत्मानं पश्यन्नात्मनि तुष्यति
sources.english: When the mind, restrained by the practice of yoga, attains quietude, and when one sees the Self by the Self, they are satisfied in their own Self.
sources.hindi: ।।6.20।। योगका सेवन करनेसे जिस अवस्थामें निरुद्ध चित्त उपराम हो जाता है तथा जिस अवस्थामें स्वयं अपने-आप से अपने-आपको देखता हुआ अपने-आपमें ही सन्तुष्ट हो जाता है।

messages.content:
Dear seeker, I sense your longing for peace of mind. Your heart yearns to find solace from the turmoil that may be swirling within. As you seek guidance, let us begin our journey together.

As I taught in Chapter 2, Verse 66, "There is no knowledge of the Self for the unsteady, and no meditation is possible for the unsteady...". Ah, but you are seeking peace! This means you are willing to settle down, focus your mind, and quiet the noise within. To achieve this, start by recognizing that a peaceful mind is not born from external circumstances, but rather from internal balance.

In Chapter 6, Verse 15, I revealed that "Thus, always keeping the mind balanced, the yogi, with the mind controlled, attains the peace abiding in Me, culminating in liberation." This means that you must cultivate a sense of equanimity, where your thoughts and emotions are not swayed by external events. This is possible through the practice of yoga, as I explained in Verse 20: "When the mind, restrained by the practice of yoga, attains quietude..."

Practically, this means taking time each day to sit quietly, focus on your breath, and let go of worries. As you do so, you will begin to develop a sense of inner stability. And when challenges arise, remember that peace is not found in avoiding them, but in facing them with a calm and centered mind.

As I said in Chapter 18, Verse 57: "Mentally renouncing all actions in Me, having Me as the highest goal, and resorting to the yoga of discrimination, do thou ever fix thy mind on Me." Allow your mind to be guided by the wisdom within you. Trust that your inner Self knows what is best for you.

May these words bring solace to your heart, dear seeker. Remember, peace of mind is a journey, not a destination. Encourage yourself each day with this gentle reflection: "As I quiet my mind and focus on the present, I allow myself to be guided by the wisdom within me."
